slides.title: Your immune system *ages too*.
slides.sub: A small gland behind your breastbone trains your defenses. As it shrinks, infections hit harder. Here is how to slow it.

slides.title: The thymus runs the show
slides.body: The thymus trains fresh T-cells, the soldiers that recognize new threats. After your twenties it *slowly shrinks* and quiets down.

slides.title: Fewer fresh defenders
slides.body: With fewer new T-cells, the immune system leans on tired old ones. This aging, called immunosenescence, *is linked to* harsher infections.

slides.title: Muscle feeds immunity
slides.body: Skeletal muscle stores the amino acids your immune cells burn during a fight. Strength work *may help* keep that reserve full.

slides.title: Sleep rebuilds defenses
slides.body: Deep sleep is when immune memory consolidates. A few short nights *can blunt* your response, even to a vaccine.

slides.title: Vaccines do the training
slides.body: When fresh T-cells are scarce, vaccines teach your existing cells the targets. They *can keep* common infections from turning serious.

slides.title: Age slower from the *inside out*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, movement, food and steps that support a resilient immune system, all in one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your immune system gets older right alongside you, and the gland that trains it quietly shrinks. 🌿

Muscle, deep sleep and staying up to date on vaccines can slow that immune aging and keep ordinary infections from becoming serious. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Your Immune System Ages, and How to Slow It
